description: Michaels Cue Bids and Unusual 2NT: compete over opponent's suit opening with two-suited hands (5-5+)
purpose: Show a two-suited hand (5-5 or better) when an opponent opens a suit, enabling partner to choose the best fit at a competitive level
scopeNote: Direct-seat Michaels cuebids and Unusual 2NT only (not balancing, not after interference). Shape map: Michaels over minor = both majors; Michaels over major = other major + unspecified minor; Unusual 2NT over 1C = D+H; over 1D = C+H; over 1M = both minors. Single strength tier encoded as `7+` with playing-strength / vulnerability handled qualitatively. The weak-vs-strong Michaels split, typed `overcall.twoSuitedShape` partition, invitational minor-jump surfaces after exact-suit Unusual 2NT (`4D` / `4C`), and the tight `3+` preference thresholds after exact-suit advancer rebids are deferred to future variant work.

authority.label: Karen Walker — Michaels cuebid and Unusual 2NT overcalls with advancer responses

snapshot.text:
# Michaels Cuebids & Unusual Notrump Overcalls

After an opponent opens, it's often difficult to describe a hand with two long suits. The auction may get too high (or end too soon) for you to show both, or you may not have the strength to safely bid either suit.

## Michaels Cuebid

This is a direct cuebid of the opponent's opened suit to show 5+-card length in two other suits. One of your suits is always a major, but the exact two you promise depend on the opening bid:

- Over a minor-suit opening (1C-2C or 1D-2D), it shows both majors.
- Over a major-suit opening (1H-2H or 1S-2S), it shows the other major and an unspecified minor.

The Michaels Cuebid replaces the Goren-style Strong Cuebid. If you have a very strong hand, you must start with a takeout double.

## Unusual 2NT

This convention is a direct jump to 2NT over an opponent's opening bid to show "the two lower-ranking unbid suits." You should have at least 5 cards in each suit:

- Over a 1C opening, 2NT shows diamonds and hearts.
- Over a 1D opening, 2NT shows clubs and hearts.
- Over a 1H or 1S opening, 2NT shows both minors.

## Strength Requirements

The strength you promise is about 7+ points, but "suit quality and playing strength are more important than high-card points." These overcalls are usually preemptive in nature.

Vulnerability affects your decision. A vulnerable overcall "should promise more playing strength" than a non-vulnerable one.

## Responding to Partner's Bid

"Remember that partner's Michaels Cuebid or 2NT overcall is artificial." If your RHO passes, you cannot pass. Bid your longer suit among partner's two offerings based on your strength and support level.

If partner makes a Michaels Cuebid over a major opening (showing the other major and an unspecified minor), you can bid 2NT to ask which minor he holds, signaling that as your preferred trump suit.

teaching.tradeoff: Michaels/Unusual 2NT sacrifices the natural meaning of the cue bid and 2NT in exchange for immediately showing a two-suited competitive hand.
teaching.principle: Against a suit opening, showing two suits at once lets partner judge fit quickly. The specific cue bid varies by opening suit to encode which suits you hold.

commonMistakes.text: Do not confuse a cue-bid of opener's suit with a Western Cue Bid.
commonMistakes.reason: Michaels uses the cue-bid to show a two-suiter, while a Western Cue asks partner for a stopper for notrump.

commonMistakes.text: Do not forget that 2NT over major-opening Michaels is a relay.
commonMistakes.reason: After Michaels over a major, advancer's 2NT asks overcaller to name the hidden minor rather than playing as a natural notrump bid.

commonMistakes.text: Do not bid Michaels with only 5-4 shape.
commonMistakes.reason: The convention promises at least 5-5 shape, so stretching on 5-4 gives partner a false picture of the hand.

explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Does not hold 5+ cards in both majors
Does not hold 5+ cards in both minors
Does not hold 5+ spades with a 5+ minor
Does not hold 5+ hearts with a 5+ minor
The minor suit is not clubs
The minor suit is not diamonds
Fewer than 3 hearts to support partner
Fewer than 3 spades to support partner
Fewer than 3 clubs to support partner
Fewer than 3 diamonds to support partner

explanationEntries.displayText: 2+ hearts — minimum to accept partner's known major or prefer hearts at the lowest level
2+ spades — minimum to accept partner's known major or prefer spades at the lowest level
2NT: Ask partner to show the minor
2NT: Ask partner to show the minor (over 1S Michaels)
4C: Raise clubs after the relay reveal
4D: Raise diamonds after the relay reveal
5C: Sacrifice in clubs after the relay reveal
5D: Sacrifice in diamonds after the relay reveal

summaryCard.trigger: Opponent opens a suit and you make a conventional two-suited overcall
summaryCard.partnership: Strength is partnership-dependent. Common styles are weak or preemptive only, roughly six to eleven HCP; weak-or-strong mini-maxi methods where intermediate hands overcall naturally; or strong-only treatments. Confirm the agreement and vulnerability discipline before bidding. Over a minor opening, Michaels shows both majors and Unusual two notrump shows the two lower unbid suits (one clubs = diamonds and hearts, one diamonds = clubs and hearts). Over a major opening, Michaels shows the other major plus an undisclosed minor and Unusual two notrump shows both minors.

whenToUse.gloss: Against a minor opening, use Michaels to show both majors at once instead of overcalling just one of them.
Against a major opening, use the cue-bid to show the other major plus a minor so partner can choose between the known major and the hidden minor.
Use unusual two notrump with the two lower unbid suits: over one clubs it shows diamonds and hearts, over one diamonds clubs and hearts, and over a major both minors.

workedAuctions.label: Minor opening: cue-bid shows both majors immediately

calls.rationale: The opponents start with a natural diamond opening.
Michaels over 1♦ shows both majors with a 5-5 style hand.
No immediate action from opener's partner.
Advancer prefers spades and places the contract in the known fit.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: a five-four hand should not pretend to be Michaels

calls.rationale: This call would promise spades plus a minor, so making it on only 5-4 shape would overstate the hand.
Partner is entitled to choose spades expecting a full two-suiter, which is why 5-4 is the wrong hand for the convention.

items.opponentAction: The opponents double the cue-bid or the unusual two notrump call
items.ourAction: Keep the artificial two-suited meaning and let advancer choose a strain unless your partnership has a specific escape gadget.
items.note: The fixture treats the original call as still conventional; doubles do not turn it natural.

items.opponentAction: The opener's side raises the opening suit aggressively
items.ourAction: Compete on fit or extra playing strength, usually by choosing the known fit rather than probing further.
items.note: Preemptive raises take away relay room, especially after a major-opening Michaels cue-bid.

items.recommendation: With a weak-to-intermediate hand, choose one of partner's suits at the lowest available level; after a relay reveal, you may pass the minor partner names.
items.note: Walker explicitly forbids passing immediately after partner's artificial Michaels cuebid or Unusual two notrump when RHO has passed.

items.recommendation: With invitational values, raise the known fit, or ask for the hidden minor only when advancing a major-opening Michaels cue-bid.
items.note: Minor-opening Michaels already shows both majors, so advancer chooses between known suits instead of asking for a better major. Over a major-opening Michaels cue-bid, the relay is only for hands that cannot support the known major and expect at least a three-three minor fit.

items.recommendation: With game values, jump to game in the known fit or, after a major-opening Michaels cue-bid, relay before committing to partner's minor.
items.note: The only authored relay is the major-opening Michaels path where partner's minor is still unknown. Natural side-suit advances, natural notrump continuations, balancing-seat uses, and higher-level unusual-notrump applications stay outside this direct-seat teaching fixture.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Michaels shows five-five two-suiters over a suit opening; DONT is specifically a defense to an opponent's one-notrump opening.
When your hand is too one-suited or too strong for the artificial two-suited overcall, fall back to natural competitive action instead of stretching for the unusual two notrump branch.

teaching.purpose: Show a two-suited hand (5-5 or better) when an opponent opens a suit, enabling partner to choose the best fit at a competitive level
teaching.whenToUse: After opponent opens 1C/1D/1H/1S: cue-bid their suit to show a two-suited hand, or bid 2NT to show the two lower unbid suits. Over a minor opening, the cue bid shows both majors and Unusual 2NT shows diamonds+hearts over 1C or clubs+hearts over 1D. Over a major, the cue bid shows the other major plus an unspecified minor and 2NT shows both minors.
whenNotToUse: With only 5-4 shape — need 5-5 or better
With a strong balanced hand — don't confuse with a Western Cue Bid
teaching.tradeoff: Michaels sacrifices the natural/strong meaning of the cue bid and 2NT in exchange for immediately showing a two-suited competitive hand
teaching.principle: Showing two suits simultaneously lets partner judge fit quickly. The vulnerability-gated HCP range (looser NV, tighter Vul) reflects the risk/reward of competing.
teaching.roles: Overcaller describes shape; advancer evaluates fit and chooses the best strain and level

definitions.description: The minor suit is clubs (clubs >= diamonds)
The minor suit is diamonds (diamonds > clubs)
HCP sufficient for a Michaels overcall (NV: 6+, Vul: 8+); no upper bound — Walker allows strong two-suiters that jump to game after partner's preference
2+ hearts — minimum support to accept partner's known major or to choose hearts at the lowest level
2+ spades — minimum support to accept partner's known major or to choose spades at the lowest level
